summary: Family history can identify some inherited forms of high cholesterol due to their predictable patterns but does not reliably predict most childhood lipid abnormalities or catch all missed diagnoses. Universal screening should be considered rather than relying solely on family history when selecting students for testing because it ensures early detection regardless of background factors.
